checks: The track visually consists of multiple connected segments with at least 2 visible turns/curves — it is clearly not a straight line but curves left and right as it extends away from the starting position

The track has a visible downhill slope — the starting area is noticeably higher/elevated compared to the end of the track, and the ball rolls forward without requiring pitch input due to gravity on the slope
A checkerboard-patterned finish line (alternating black and white squares) is visible spanning the full width of the track at the end of the course
When the ball reaches and crosses the finish line, an overlay appears with the text 'COURSE COMPLETE!' along with the player's score, indicating the run has ended successfully
The ball physics correctly follow the curved track surface — as the track turns, the ball follows the curve and stays on the surface without clipping through or floating above the track geometry
The camera smoothly rotates and follows the track heading as the ball moves through turns — the camera view turns with the track direction rather than always facing one fixed direction
Red box obstacles are placed along the curved track surface (not floating off the edges or in empty space) and colliding with them still triggers game over / ball falling off
Gold torus-shaped coins are visible along the curved track and can be collected to increment the score counter in the top-left corner
The green turtle powerup is visible on the curved track and collecting it still triggers the 'SLOWED' indicator and visibly reduces ball speed
The ball can still fall off the edge of the track if the player tilts too far laterally, preserving the core challenge — the track has visible edges
After game over or course completion and restart, the game properly resets with a fresh layout of obstacles, coins, and turtle on the curved track
